catalysts_general: HBM3E 100% sold out through 2026; HBM4 ramping Q2 2026; DA Davidson outlier target $1,000 vs median analyst $549 (BELOW current $646) (T1: Micron IR, TIKR, TrendForce)

Discovery: HBM3E capacity 100% sold out through calendar 2026; Q1 FY26 revenue +57% YoY with gross margins expanding to 50%+ (from 22% in FY24); data center now 56% of revenue; DRAM contract prices forecast +55-60% QoQ in Q1 2026 as AI reallocation of wafer capacity creates structural shortage across conventional DRAM too.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI HBM supercycle is real but consensus median target 15% below current $646; wait for pullback to $580-620 zone before initiating

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: VLCC spot rates ATH $423,736/day; Q1 TCEs $107,100/day +44%; FRO +64% YTD, analyst mean $37.67 slightly BELOW current $38.12 (T4: 24/7 Wall St., Barchart)

Discovery: Hormuz closure since Feb 28 forces global tanker rerouting, dramatically extending voyage miles and pushing war-risk insurance premiums 2-3x higher; FRO shares up ~64% YTD as tanker day-rates surge — even partial Hormuz reopening would sustain elevated insurance premiums as a new baseline, extending the earnings cycle.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Hormuz tailwind largely priced at +64% YTD and trading near 52W high; wait for pullback to $33-37 before initiating

catalysts_general: Q1 net sales $2.65B +30%; adj. EPS $1.17 +83%; FY guide raised to $13.5-14B; backlog $15B doubled YoY; analyst target $347 only +2% from current $340 (T2: Vertiv IR)

Discovery: Q1 2026 adjusted earnings +83% YoY, backlog above $15B (more than doubled last year), raised 2026 revenue guidance to $13.5-14B with 30% organic growth and 51% EPS growth; liquid cooling deployments surpassing 1 GW, positioning Vertiv as the dominant power-and-cooling vendor as rack density hits 120-150kW for AI workloads.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Dominant AI power-and-cooling leader with $15B backlog and 83% EPS growth but analyst consensus implies near-zero near-term upside from current $340

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: FY2026 ARR $5.25B; FY2027 revenue guided $5.87-5.93B +22-23%; Wedbush top pick; analyst consensus flat to current; Iranian cyberwar escalation real tailwind (T2: CrowdStrike IR)

Discovery: Iran's cyberwarfare campaign (Electronic Operations Room formed Feb 28, 2026) triggered a joint CISA/NSA/Cyber Command/FBI advisory on APT attacks against US water, energy, and government infrastructure; CrowdStrike Falcon detected Russian proxy groups supporting Iran targeting US industry, driving emergency government contract demand for its endpoint-AI platform.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Dominant cybersecurity platform with Iranian cyberwar tailwind but Fwd P/E 82 and analyst consensus AT current $505 leaves no margin of safety
